behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module boundary (CSV row reader + schema validator + DLQ publisher + row index tracking), not just one function. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network requirement if DLQ sink is local test double/storage adapter. MOCK-HIDE: pure unit tests can miss row-number propagation and wiring bugs between validator and DLQ writer. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Run the row-processing pipeline components together on a small CSV fixture with known missing fields at specific line numbers. Use a real in-process DLQ adapter (e.g., local table/file/in-memory repository used by production code path), then assert rejected payload contains exact original row numbers and raw row content.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is transformation/parsing logic in one boundary. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: mocking adds no value; real failure is parser ambiguity and normalization rules. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Call the date-normalization function directly with a table of format variants (including ambiguous and invalid edge cases) and assert canonical output or explicit validation failure.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is a runtime/resource failure boundary across full pipeline behavior. REAL-INFRA: depends on actual process memory use, I/O throughput, and timeout enforcement. MOCK-HIDE: mocked I/O or reduced dataset can hide buffering/chunking leaks and GC pressure. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline as a real process against a generated 500k-row CSV and real backing services used in test environment, with production-like timeout config. Capture wall-clock duration and peak RSS; fail if timeout exceeded or memory crosses budget/OOM occurs.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at external API interaction boundary (S3 client + retry policy + batch controller). REAL-INFRA: to catch real SDK/HTTP retry behavior and backoff timing, use real HTTP interaction (e.g., S3-compatible endpoint/fault-injecting server). MOCK-HIDE: simple mocks often bypass middleware and make retries appear correct when integration is wrong. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against an S3-compatible test endpoint that returns 503 for first N GET attempts and then succeeds. Verify request attempt count and increasing delay intervals, and confirm batch continues and processes rows after recovery.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: idempotency-on-restart spans writer logic, transaction boundaries, checkpoints, and restart semantics. REAL-INFRA: requires real DB behavior plus process restart/crash-recovery behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked DB or no real restart can miss duplicate inserts due to commit/checkpoint ordering races. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Start pipeline on a deterministic fixture with unique business keys, force termination after partial commit, then restart with same input and checkpoint state. Use real PostgreSQL constraints/upsert path and assert final row count equals unique input rows with no duplicates.
